title: Analyses of bottom simulating reflections offshore Arauco and Coyhaique (Chile)
abstract: Two seismic sections offshore Arauco (~37°S) and Coyhaique (~44°S) in the Chilean continental margin were analyzed to characterize hydrate-bearing sediments. Velocity analysis was used to estimate gas-phase concentrations and identify hydrate and free gas layers relative to the BSR. In the southern sector (Coyhaique), maximum gas hydrate and free gas concentrations were calculated at 15% and 2.7% of total volume respectively.

location.description: Two sites on the Chilean margin: offshore Arauco (~37°S) and offshore Coyhaique (~44°S)

data.name: MCS Sections — Arauco (~37°S) and Coyhaique (~44°S) Offshore Chile
data.description: Two seismic sections from the Chilean margin analyzed for BSR velocity and gas hydrate/free gas quantification: offshore Arauco (~37°S) and Coyhaique (~44°S); maximum concentrations 15% hydrate and 2.7% free gas at Coyhaique. [SPOC cruise (SO161, 2001) datasets searchable on PANGAEA]

_data_note: Geo-Marine Letters paper; behind paywall. Uses Chilean margin seismic data, likely from SPOC/GEOMAR or Conrad cruises.

processing.summary: Velocity analysis was applied to two MCS seismic sections from the Chilean margin at Arauco (~37°S) and Coyhaique (~44°S) to derive BSR-based gas hydrate and free gas estimates. The Coyhaique sector shows higher concentrations (hydrate: 15%, free gas: 2.7% of total volume). Results compare BSR characteristics in two contrasting tectonic settings on the south-central Chilean margin.
workflow: 4. Gas hydrate and free gas concentration estimation
processing.notes: Two-site comparison paper; sister paper to other Vargas-Cordero Coyhaique study (2010)

analysis_notes: Geo-Marine Letters paper; behind paywall. Uses Chilean margin seismic profiles from legacy cruises.
